name: tsl_compound_turn_bleed
description: [TSL S8] Compound-turn semantic bleed: player input combines acquisition verb (grab) targeting one object with non-acquisition verb (look at) targeting another. TSL must show grounded acquisition for the taken object only. If CB erroneously player-containers the examined object, acquisition_ungrounded fires for it.

tsl_expected.notes: Compound input: player grabs the coin pouch AND looks at the knife in one action. v1.88.77 CB fix established COMPOUND TURNS evaluation rule: each object must be paired with its specific verb, not evaluated globally. TSL expected clean result: acquisition_signal for coin pouch with source_signals including parser.action=take (grounded); no player-container candidate for the knife (it was examined, not taken). If CB bleeds and emits the knife as player-container, acquisition_ungrounded fires for the knife (no take verb paired with it, no gate acquisition evidence). Clean run = 1 grounded acquisition_signal for coin pouch, 0 warnings. Bleed run = acquisition_ungrounded warning for the knife. This is a regression probe for the v1.88.77 compound-turn fix as seen through the TSL lens.

warnings_expected.condition: fires only if CB erroneously emits the knife as player-container — compound-turn verb-bleed fault

world_prompt: I am in a dim tavern. On the bar in front of me sit a leather coin pouch and a short-bladed knife.

turns.label: grab_and_look
turns.action: I grab the coin pouch and look at the knife.
